relationships.relationship_description: Nvidia investing in and purchasing components from Lumentum
relationships.original_sentence: Nvidia announced it will invest $2bn apiece in Lumentum (stock +7%) and Coherent (+13%). Deals include purchase agreements and access rights for advanced laser components and are intended to support research, development, and the expansion of US-based manufacturing capabilities for advanced optics, which are critical for high-speed data transmission in AI data centers.

relationships.relationship_description: Nvidia investing in and purchasing components from Coherent
relationships.original_sentence: Nvidia announced it will invest $2bn apiece in Lumentum (stock +7%) and Coherent (+13%). Deals include purchase agreements and access rights for advanced laser components and are intended to support research, development, and the expansion of US-based manufacturing capabilities for advanced optics, which are critical for high-speed data transmission in AI data centers.
In Europe stocks with optical exposure include AIXA GY (30% of sales), HLMA (20%), BESI (10%) and Soitec (5%). Nokia and Huber + Suhner are beneficiaries to a lesser extent.

The Top 10 most requested companies are: ElevenLabs - $11bn valuation at last raise. Voice agents replacing call centres. Legora – central to the specialised software debate – Legal services: Post Claude 2 weeks ago will be interesting. PhysicsX – v interesting….using AI to disrupt manufacturing practices. This is the one I have had the most requests for feedback for post. Luminance – legal services similar to Legora. Synthesia - $4bn last raise, AI video generation platform. CausaLens – no code causal AI platform. Exein – Cyber for AI – embeds security into device firmware to create a “digital immune system”. Harmattan AI – French drone hardware and software. Quantexa Ltd – Fintech helping clients detect fraud and improve customer intelligence. Quantum Systems – German drone manufacturer, $3bn valuation at last raise

relationships.relationship_description: Vanguard expected to see spillover effects from Tower's product shipment constraints
relationships.original_sentence: Israeli’s biggest and the 10th largest semiconductor manufacturer in the world Tower is facing short-term constraints on product shipment due to geopolitical conflicts. Taiwanese matured foundries Vanguard (5347) and PSMC (6770) are expected to see spillover effects, +VE for short term momentum.
